meta.title: About | ScaletoTop
meta.description: The ScaletoTop methodology: internally driven, governing semantics with business logic. Every piece of content becomes a measurable asset.

about.heroHeadline: Why does 99% of content burn money?
about.heroHighlight: When results are on the line, volume was never a moat.
about.heroDescription: In the age of AI content floods, we don't chase "more" — we chase more complete logic. Every piece of content should be a measurable asset, not a sunk cost.
about.philosophyHeadline: Internally driven: govern semantics with business logic

principles.title: Business DNA extraction
principles.description: Every great business has its own axiomatic system. We use AI to deconstruct your core business, audience pain points, and brand voice, so the content we produce carries native commercial instinct.

principles.title: Semantic authority
principles.description: Real expertise isn't covering your competitor's keywords — it's completing your own knowledge system. When your content logic is airtight, search engines have no choice but to put you on top.

principles.title: Traffic arbitrage radar
principles.description: Replace ad spend with compute. Lock onto organic ranking opportunities for high-CPC keywords and win asymmetrically. Every dollar of budget compounds into long-term digital assets.

about.techDescription: STP isn't a toolbox — it's an organic system that monitors and corrects itself. From Site Intelligence reconnaissance to GEO Writer execution, it eliminates the cognitive load between spotting an opportunity and shipping the result.
about.ctaHeadline: Ready to make your content work for you?
about.ctaDescription: Analyze your site and find the content gaps search engines are ignoring. Free to start, no credit card required.

MISSING_SITEMAP.title: Missing Sitemap
MISSING_SITEMAP.explanation: No valid sitemap.xml was found in the root directory. Sitemaps help search engines discover and crawl your pages more efficiently.
MISSING_SITEMAP.howToFix: Generate a sitemap.xml and place it in the root directory, then submit it in Google Search Console.

DEAD_LINK.title: Dead Link
DEAD_LINK.explanation: The page returned a 4xx or 5xx error. Dead links harm user experience and block search engine crawling.
DEAD_LINK.howToFix: Fix these links to ensure they return 200 OK, or set up 301 redirects.

MISSING_H1.title: Missing H1 Tag
MISSING_H1.explanation: The H1 tag is the most important heading, telling search engines and users the core topic of the page.
MISSING_H1.howToFix: Add a descriptive H1 tag to every page.

TITLE_TOO_LONG.title: Title Too Long
TITLE_TOO_LONG.explanation: Page title exceeds 60 characters and may be truncated in search results.
TITLE_TOO_LONG.howToFix: Shorten the title, ensure core keywords are at the beginning, and keep it under 60 characters.

TITLE_TOO_SHORT.title: Title Too Short
TITLE_TOO_SHORT.explanation: The title is too short to provide enough semantic context for ranking.
TITLE_TOO_SHORT.howToFix: Expand the title with more relevant descriptive words.

MISSING_TITLE.title: Missing Title
MISSING_TITLE.explanation: The page has no <title> tag defined.
MISSING_TITLE.howToFix: Add an engaging, keyword-rich title to the page.

MISSING_META_DESC.title: Missing Meta Description
MISSING_META_DESC.explanation: Meta descriptions don't directly affect ranking but significantly impact Click-Through Rate (CTR).
MISSING_META_DESC.howToFix: Write an engaging description of 120-160 characters for each page.

META_DESC_TOO_LONG.title: Meta Description Too Long
META_DESC_TOO_LONG.explanation: Descriptions over 160 characters will be truncated by search engines.
META_DESC_TOO_LONG.howToFix: Concatenate the description, ensuring the most important info is at the front.

DUPLICATE_H1.title: Multiple H1 Tags
DUPLICATE_H1.explanation: A page should only have one H1 tag. Multiple H1s may confuse search engines about the page's topic.
DUPLICATE_H1.howToFix: Ensure only one H1 tag per page; change others to H2 or H3.

DUPLICATE_TITLE.title: Duplicate Title
DUPLICATE_TITLE.explanation: Multiple pages using the exact same title makes it hard for search engines to distinguish which is most relevant.
DUPLICATE_TITLE.howToFix: Provide unique, targeted titles for every page.

DUPLICATE_META_DESC.title: Duplicate Meta Description
DUPLICATE_META_DESC.explanation: Duplicate meta descriptions reduce the uniqueness of each page.
DUPLICATE_META_DESC.howToFix: Write unique meta descriptions for each page.

MISSING_OG_IMAGE.title: Missing OG Image
MISSING_OG_IMAGE.explanation: Missing Open Graph images reduces the visual appeal and CTR when shared on social media (Twitter, LinkedIn).
MISSING_OG_IMAGE.howToFix: Add an og:image tag in the <head>.

THIN_CONTENT.title: Thin Content
THIN_CONTENT.explanation: The page has fewer than 300 words and may be viewed as low quality by search engines.
THIN_CONTENT.howToFix: Add more high-quality, in-depth original content.

SLOW_PAGE.title: Slow Loading
SLOW_PAGE.explanation: Page load time exceeds 3s, which can increase bounce rates.
SLOW_PAGE.howToFix: Optimize image sizes, reduce redirects, or use a CDN.

VERY_SLOW_PAGE.title: Very Slow Loading
VERY_SLOW_PAGE.explanation: Page load time exceeds 5s, severely impacting experience and ranking.
VERY_SLOW_PAGE.howToFix: Focus on optimization, checking server response times or heavy scripts.

MISSING_CANONICAL.title: Missing Canonical Tag
MISSING_CANONICAL.explanation: Missing canonical tags can lead to ambiguity when search engines handle URL parameters or duplicate content.
MISSING_CANONICAL.howToFix: Add a rel="canonical" link to every page.

ORPHAN_PAGE.title: Orphan Page
ORPHAN_PAGE.explanation: These pages have no internal links pointing to them. It's hard for search engines and users to discover them.
ORPHAN_PAGE.howToFix: Add internal links to these pages from other relevant parts of your site.

MISSING_H2.title: Missing H2 Tag
MISSING_H2.explanation: The page has long content but lacks H2 headings for structure, making it harder to read and understand.
MISSING_H2.howToFix: Use H2 tags to add subheadings and improve readability.

HEADING_HIERARCHY_BROKEN.title: Heading Hierarchy Jump
HEADING_HIERARCHY_BROKEN.explanation: The heading hierarchy is not sequential (e.g., H1 followed by H3). Good structure should be progressive.
HEADING_HIERARCHY_BROKEN.howToFix: Adjust heading levels to ensure H2 is used before H3.

MISSING_VIEWPORT.title: Missing Viewport Setting
MISSING_VIEWPORT.explanation: Missing viewport meta tag means the page won't scale correctly on mobile devices.
MISSING_VIEWPORT.howToFix: Add <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"> to the <head>.

MISSING_STRUCTURED_DATA.title: Missing Structured Data
MISSING_STRUCTURED_DATA.explanation: No JSON-LD structured data was found. Structured data helps show rich snippets in search results.
MISSING_STRUCTURED_DATA.howToFix: Add appropriate Schema.org JSON-LD markup based on content type (Article, Product, etc.).
